segments.text: Job replied,
Yes, I know all that! But how can anyone be right before God?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If you wanted to argue with God, God could ask a thousand questions that no one could answer.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: God is so wise and so powerful that no one could challenge him and win.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: God moves the mountains suddenly; he overturns them in his anger.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He shakes the earth, making its foundations quake.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He is the one who can command the sun not to rise and the stars not to shine.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He alone is the one who stretches out the heavens and walks on the waves of the sea.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He made the constellations of the Bear, Orion, the Pleiades, and the stars of the southern sky.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He is the one who does incredible things that are beyond our understanding, marvelous things that are uncountable.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But when he passes by me, I don't see him; when he moves on, he is invisible to me.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: If he takes away,  who can prevent him? Who is going to ask him, ‘What are you doing?’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: God does not restrain his anger; he crushes Rahab's helpers underfoot.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: So how much less could I answer God, or choose my words to argue with him!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Even though I'm right,  I can't answer him. I must plead for mercy from my judge.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Even if I called him to come and he responded, I don't believe he would listen to me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He pounds me with the winds of a storm; he wounds me time and again, without giving a reason.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He doesn't give me a chance even to catch my breath; instead he fills my life with bitter suffering.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If it's a question of strength, then God is the strongest. If it's a question of justice, then who will set a time for my case?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Even though I am right, my own mouth would condemn me; even though I am innocent, he would prove me wrong.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I am innocent! I don't care what happens to me. I hate my life!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: That's why I say, ‘It makes no difference to God. He destroys both the innocent and the wicked.’
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: When disaster strikes suddenly he mocks the despair of the innocent.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: The earth has been handed over to the wicked; he blinds the eyes of the judges—if it's not him who does this, then who is it?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: The days of my life race by like a runner, rushing past without me seeing any happiness.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: They pass by like fast sailing ships, like an eagle swooping down on its prey.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If I said to myself, ‘I will forget my complaints; I will stop crying and be happy,’
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I would still be terrified at all my suffering because you, God, will not say I'm innocent.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Since I'm condemned, what's the point in arguing?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Even if I wash myself with pure mountain water and clean my hands with soap,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: you would toss me into a slime pit so that even my own clothes would hate me!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: For God is not a mortal being like me, I can't defend myself or take him to court.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If only there was an arbitrator who could bring us both together!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I wish God would stop beating me with his rod and terrifying me!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Then I could speak up without being afraid—but since I am, I can't!
segments.speaker: Job
